summary: Current ecosystem packaging truth for the public OpenUI MCP Studio repo. The repo is the main distribution artifact, while install bundles, the repo-owned skill product line, and supporting parked lanes explain how different clients consume that repo today.

currentTruth.codex: The public repo ships a repo-owned Codex install surface through MCP configuration, starter bundles, proof loop, and troubleshooting.
currentTruth.claudeCode: The public repo ships a repo-owned Claude Code install surface through MCP configuration, starter bundles, proof loop, and troubleshooting.
currentTruth.genericMcpHost: A reusable stdio MCP launch template is documented for other MCP-capable hosts.
currentTruth.formalSkills: The repo-owned skill product line is current through @openui/skills-kit plus the repo mirror under examples/skills.
currentTruth.pluginLikePackaging: Codex and Claude Code install bundles are supporting install surfaces for the public repo, not separate primary products.
currentTruth.openclawBridge: The repo-owned OpenClaw skill line is current through starter bundles, install/proof loop, and discoverable contract artifacts, without claiming listing approval or runtime verification.
currentTruth.sdk: Supporting or parked package surface through @openui/sdk.
currentTruth.hostedApi: Supporting or parked self-hosted runtime through the repo CLI and hosted API contract.

clientSupportMatrix.why: The public repo documents a repo-owned Codex install surface through local stdio MCP, starter bundles, proof loop, and troubleshooting.
clientSupportMatrix.notFor: Claiming a Codex marketplace item or any vendor-approved plugin listing.

clientSupportMatrix.why: The public repo documents a repo-owned Claude Code install surface through local stdio MCP, starter bundles, proof loop, and troubleshooting.
clientSupportMatrix.notFor: Claiming a Claude Code plugin listing or managed hosted runtime.

clientSupportMatrix.why: The repo documents a reusable stdio launch contract for MCP-capable hosts beyond Codex and Claude Code.
clientSupportMatrix.notFor: Claiming vendor-native verification, a marketplace listing, or a host-specific support promise.

clientSupportMatrix.why: The repo compares itself against broader coding-agent traffic but does not ship a dedicated install or discovery path for OpenHands today.
clientSupportMatrix.notFor: Claiming current install-ready packaging or an official OpenHands integration.

clientSupportMatrix.why: OpenCode can reuse the same repo-owned generic MCP launch contract as another MCP-first client, but the repo does not claim a dedicated OpenCode install shelf.
clientSupportMatrix.notFor: Claiming a dedicated OpenCode install surface or an official OpenCode integration.

clientSupportMatrix.why: The repo now exposes an official repo-owned OpenClaw skill product line through starter configs, public starter pack artifacts, proof loops, and discoverable contracts. That is still not an official listing or verified runtime lane.
clientSupportMatrix.notFor: Claiming a verified OpenClaw runtime, ClawHub listing, marketplace item, or official plugin/skills publication.

surfaces.title: Official repo-owned skill product line
surfaces.audience: maintainers and builders who need a versioned, repo-owned skill product line before any future listing or vendor approval exists
surfaces.role: installable skill product line with repo mirror, starter bundles, proof loops, and bounded contract examples
surfaces.packageShape: public package @openui/skills-kit plus repo mirror under examples/skills; current as an unlisted official repo-owned skill surface
surfaces.notFor: claiming a shipped public Skills runtime, marketplace item, or hosted integration platform

surfaces.title: Codex and Claude supporting install bundles
surfaces.audience: Codex, Claude Code, and other MCP-capable hosts that can spawn local stdio servers
surfaces.role: configuration snippets, starter bundles, CLI guidance, proof loop, and troubleshooting that help people use the public repo through local MCP installation
surfaces.packageShape: supporting install shelf for the public repo; not a listed marketplace artifact and not a separate primary product
surfaces.notFor: claiming a Codex marketplace item, a published Claude Code plugin, or vendor-native verification for every MCP-capable host

surfaces.title: OpenClaw repo-owned skill product line
surfaces.audience: OpenClaw-side builders and operators who need a discoverable install/proof path before any official listing exists
surfaces.role: repo-owned OpenClaw skill product line with sample config, proof loop, and discoverable artifacts
surfaces.packageShape: skill bundle plus contracts and docs for an unlisted OpenClaw-ready artifact, not an official listing artifact
surfaces.notFor: claiming a verified OpenClaw runtime, ClawHub listing, or official marketplace approval

surfaces.title: Hosted client SDK
surfaces.audience: developers evaluating future thin-client or package surfaces
surfaces.role: thin client for the self-hosted OpenUI Hosted API kept as a supporting lane
surfaces.notFor: claiming registry publication, front-door primary status, replacing the local stdio MCP runtime, or implying managed SaaS

surfaces.title: Self-hosted OpenUI Hosted API
surfaces.audience: adapter authors and future hosted-surface planners
surfaces.role: self-hosted HTTP runtime surfaced through the root CLI and retained as a supporting lane
surfaces.packageShape: repo CLI entrypoint plus internal hosted-api source package
surfaces.notFor: claiming a managed SaaS deployment, front-door primary status, control plane, or remote write surface

operatorOnlyActions: future GitHub Homepage override beyond the current Pages front door
GitHub Social Preview selection and verification
publishing releases and refreshing attached public assets
domain, DNS, TLS, and hosted infrastructure setup
